mnemonics.title: WIREFRAME Purpose
mnemonics.content:
W - Wire (skeleton structure)
I - Iterate quickly (early validation)
R - Research-based (from user research)
E - Essential elements only
F - Focus on function not form
R - Review with stakeholders
A - Annotate interactions
M - Mockup preparation
M - Mobile-first approach
E - Early usability testing

acronyms.expansion: User Experience - Overall experience of a user when interacting with a product
acronyms.usage: Wireframes primarily focus on UX by establishing layout and navigation before visual design

acronyms.expansion: User Interface - The visual elements through which users interact with a product
acronyms.usage: UI design comes after wireframing to add visual styling to the wireframe structure

acronyms.expansion: Call-to-Action - Interactive elements prompting user action
acronyms.usage: CTAs are key wireframe elements that need clear placement and visibility testing

acronyms.expansion: Low-Fidelity - Simple, basic wireframe using sketches or rough shapes
acronyms.usage: Lo-Fi wireframes are created first for quick iteration and concept validation

acronyms.expansion: High-Fidelity - Detailed wireframe with near-final layout and basic interactivity
acronyms.usage: Hi-Fi wireframes come after lo-fi/medium-fi for detailed layout and early prototype testing

visualTips.visualization: Think of building a house: Lo-Fi = blueprint sketch; Mid-Fi = floor plan with dimensions; Hi-Fi = furnished model ready for walkthrough
Wireframe = skeleton (what's there); Mockup = dressed skeleton (how it looks); Prototype = moving mannequin (how it works)
Use grayscale to 'strip away' emotional bias - colors distract stakeholders from evaluating actual layout and functionality
Stack wireframe elements like building blocks: Desktop = side-by-side columns; Tablet = adjusted columns; Mobile = stacked single column

analogies.analogy: Just like an architect's blueprint shows room layouts before building, wireframes show page layouts before coding begins
Artists sketch before painting - designers wireframe before polishing to get structure right first
Moving furniture (UI elements) is easy on paper (wireframe); moving after building (coding) is expensive and time-consuming
